title: Base Conversion
summary: Algorithms for converting numbers between bases: base-d to decimal (formula), decimal to base-d (integer: repeated division; fractional: repeated multiplication), and shortcuts between bases that are powers of each other.
content_points: Base d → decimal: apply the positional formula N = Σ a_i * d^i.
Decimal integer → base d: divide repeatedly by d; remainders (bottom-up) give digits.
Decimal fraction → base d: multiply by d repeatedly; integer parts (top-down) give digits. May produce a repeating sequence.
If d1 = d2^k: each base-d1 digit maps to exactly k base-d2 digits (group substitution).
Hex ↔ binary: each hex digit = 4 binary digits.
learning_objectives: Convert integers and fractions between bases using the standard algorithms
Use group-substitution shortcuts for hex ↔ binary
